body:
## Problem

The 63 per-drummer lick hub pages (`/drummers/<slug>/licks`) are getting CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList schema via #1325 (approved, in ai-fix queue). However, **no FAQPage schema** is present or planned for these pages.

FAQPage is the schema type that makes AI Overviews pull structured answers. When someone asks:
- "How many lick tutorials does MetalForge have for Tomas Haake?"
- "What signature licks does [drummer] have?"

...a FAQPage on the lick hub page is the mechanism that gets MetalForge cited.

These are high-value pages: 63 × the organic traffic potential of the drummer brand.

## Fix

**File:** `api/meta/[...path].js` — the existing `/drummers/<slug>/licks` handler (or the lick hub SSR handler wherever it lives)

Add FAQPage schema to the `articleSchema` field for the lick hub response:

```js
// When generating the /drummers/<slug>/licks SSR meta response, append:
const lickCount = getSignatureLicksForDrummer(drummer).length; // look up from SIGNATURE_LICKS

articleSchema: JSON.stringify({
  '@context': 'https://schema.org',
  '@graph': [
    // ... existing CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList from #1325 ...
    {
      '@type': 'FAQPage',
      mainEntity: [
        {
          '@type': 'Question',
          name: `How many signature lick tutorials does MetalForge have for ${drummer.name}?`,
          acceptedAnswer: {
            '@type': 'Answer',
            text: `MetalForge has ${lickCount} signature lick tutorial${lickCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''} for ${drummer.name}, covering key techniques from their career.`,
          },
        },
        {
          '@type': 'Question',
          name: `Are ${drummer.name}'s lick tutorials free?`,
          acceptedAnswer: {
            '@type': 'Answer',
            text: `Yes, all ${drummer.name} lick tutorials on MetalForge are free, including video demonstrations and HowTo breakdowns.`,
          },
        },
        {
          '@type': 'Question',
          name: `What technique does ${drummer.name} focus on in their lick tutorials?`,
          acceptedAnswer: {
            '@type': 'Answer',
            text: `${drummer.name}'s lick tutorials on MetalForge cover their signature techniques used in ${drummer.band || 'their career'}. Each tutorial includes gear notes and a video demonstration.`,
          },
        },
      ],
    },
  ],
}),
```

## Pages affected
~63 pages (one per drummer) — but initially only the drummers who **have lick pages** (currently 51+). Drummers without licks can use a generic FAQ about their potential upcoming coverage.

## Expected impact
- FAQPage → AI Overview eligibility for 51+ drummer lick hub pages
- Answers "how many MetalForge lick tutorials for X" queries → direct AI citation
- Compounds CollectionPage schema from #1325

## Sequencing
**This issue should be implemented AFTER #1325 ships** (adds CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList). The FAQPage should be merged into the same `articleSchema` `@graph` array that #1325 creates — no separate schema block. Flag to implementer: sequence after #1325.

Alternatively, the implementer can combine both #1325 and this issue's schema into one PR if #1325 hasn't shipped yet.

## Quality gate
- ≥51 drummer lick hub pages serve FAQPage schema in SSR
- All Q&A text is accurate (lickCount ≥1 for drummers with licks)
- CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList from #1325 still present (no regression)
- Google Rich Results Test on a lick hub page shows FAQPage valid

## Verify
1. `curl -s https://metalforge.io/api/meta/drummers/tomas-haake/licks | grep FAQPage` returns match
2. Rich Results Test on `/drummers/tomas-haake/licks` shows FAQPage valid
3. lickCount in FAQ answer text is accurate for multiple drummers
4. No regression to CollectionPage schema (#1325)
5. Drummers with 0 licks: FAQ still present but uses generic language

## Done
- [x] FAQPage schema added to per-drummer lick hub SSR meta response
- [x] Sequenced after / merged with #1325
- [x] ≥51 lick hub pages serve FAQPage in SSR
- [x] Rich Results Test validates FAQPage

## Status
Already shipped in #1501 and #1533. FAQPage is live in `api/meta/[...path].js` lines 1094-1121 (with-licks) and 1156-1183 (no-licks). This run record confirms no regression.
title: SEO batch: /drummers/<slug>/licks hub pages — FAQPage JSON-LD for AI Overview eligibility (~63 pages)
